title: US Stock Market: Samsung shares rise after Nvidia's Huang flags tie-up on new AI chips
description: Samsung Electronics shares saw a significant jump on Tuesday. This surge followed an announcement by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang. He revealed that Samsung is manufacturing Nvidia's new artificial intelligence chips. These advanced chips are based on technology from chip startup Groq. Production is underway and shipments are expected in the latter half of this year. Samsung's manufacturing prowess is highlighted.

title: Nvidia making AI module for outer space
description: Nvidia chief Jensen Huang on Monday said the leading artificial intelligence chip maker is heading for space with a goal of powering orbiting data centers. Tech firms are floating the idea of building data centers in space and tapping into the sun's energy to meet out-of-this-world power demands in a fierce artificial intelligence race.

title: Indian insurers quiz firms on AI usage to gauge tech risks
description: Cyber insurers in India are asking companies detailed questions about their use of artificial intelligence as a condition for coverage, signalling a shift in how the industry evaluates technology risk. Underwriters now seek details on the types of AI companies deploy, how data flows through those systems, who can access AI models and whether firms have processes to detect and fix unusual outputs. Insurers are also examining deployment safeguards and how quickly companies can respond if an AI system fails.

title: IT firms rethink billing as artificial intelligence reshapes work
description: US-based IT services company EPAM Systems Inc, and homegrown Tech Mahindra Ltd are looking to include artificial intelligence costs in billing rates and charge clients based on AI usage, instead of the traditional model of billing for engineers' time, as automation tools disrupt the industry.
